title: ReviveSystem
description:
Revive players after death

Special items and mechanics

Customizable settings

Compatible with Minecraft 1.21–1.21.10
body:
**ReviveSystem**

This plugin is especially designed for Hardcore and Semi-Hardcore servers.

While death remains a serious and meaningful event, ReviveSystem introduces a controlled and limited way for players to return to the game. Revivals require teamwork, rare items, and strategic decisions, ensuring that the Hardcore feeling is preserved.

**What does the plugin do?**

When a player dies, they are not immediately eliminated but can be revived by other players using specific items or at designated locations. This creates new tactical opportunities and a more exciting gameplay experience.

**Benefits & Advantages**

Prevents frustration from permadeath

Encourages teamwork and collaboration

Introduces new gameplay mechanics and challenges

Customizable for different server types

**How does the revive system work?**

Player death: When a player dies, they enter a "waiting state" (e.g., spectator mode).

Revival: Other players can bring them back to life using a special revive item or at a respawn location.

Revive items: These items can be found, crafted, or given as rewards (see below).

Configuration: Admins can set how many lives a player has, how items are distributed, and more.

**Example Gameplay Flow**

Player A dies in combat and becomes a spectator.

Player B finds a revive item and uses it on Player A.

Player A is revived and can continue playing.

Items & Mechanics

Revive Item: A special item used for reviving players. Can be found, crafted, or granted in-game.

Respawn Point: A designated location where revivals can take place.

Head Mechanic: Optionally, player heads can act as "tokens" for revival.

**FAQ**
What happens if no one has a revive item?

The player remains in the waiting state until another player revives them or an admin intervenes.

Which servers are supported?

Bukkit, Spigot, and Paper (tested with Minecraft 1.21 to 1.21.10).

How can I test the plugin?

Install it on a test server, kill a player, and try reviving them with the revive item.

**Features**

Players can be revived after death

Special items and mechanics for the revive system

Customizable settings (e.g., number of lives, messages, items)

Compatible with Minecraft 1.21–1.21.10

Promotes teamwork and fairness

**Installation**

Download the latest version of the plugin (JAR file).

Place the JAR file in your Minecraft server's plugins folder.

Restart the server.

Adjust the configuration as needed (see above).

Configuration

Configuration files are created automatically on first server start.

Adjust settings such as number of lives, messages, items, and recipes according to your preferences.

**Compatibility**

Tested with Minecraft 1.21–1.21.10

Requires a server running Bukkit, Spigot, or Paper

**Support**

For support, feedback, or suggestions, feel free to contact me on Discord.
I am happy to help! [desertlox]

License

This plugin is not open source. All rights reserved.
